claim_results.claim: Same equation, different temperatures — the formal case.
The Structural Case for Regime-Dependent Theology Same equation, different temperatures — the formal case.
The pattern: God intervenes externally and catastrophically because there is no internal correction mechanism.
He still encounters demons directly — because He is operating in the pre-Pentecost window.
Rev 12:12 — "the devil is come down unto you, having great wrath, because he knoweth that he hath but a short time." Cast down and constrained.
The wrath is precisely because access has been limited.
The Statistical Evidence More than "Jesus cast out demons" is required.
The distribution shows a clear phase transition at the Gospels/Acts boundary.
The scale drops while the frequency increases — exactly what the distribution law predicts.
If σ = 0 means "no protection layer," then pre-Pentecost everyone was effectively σ = 0 with respect to adversarial defense.
Every protection must be structural — the Law, the Temple, the priesthood, geographic separation — rather than internal, because the internal mechanism does not yet exist.
Any claim that turns a structural resemblance into a proven mechanism belongs here until the math, experiment, or textual chain can carry the weight.
Possible overstatement: language that sounds final should be read as a working model unless the article explicitly proves it.

OT adversarial access is direct (serpent to Eve, Satan to Job, evil spirits to Saul) and divine intervention is external-catastrophic (flood, Sodom, Exodus, exile) because pre-Pentecost humans had no internal protection layer.
Rigor & Kill Conditions Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.
Every equation that appears in the paper, collected here as a reference index.
Plain English: The same God runs the equation in two regimes.
Pre-Pentecost the system needs Ĝ external — floods, fire, plagues, exile — because there is no internal coupling term.
The interesting ones are +1 (the church) and −1 (a category that didn't exist pre-Pentecost because the protection layer wasn't yet a baseline).

Constrained Wrath (Revelation 12:12) $$\frac{d|\text{adv}|}{dt}\!\to\! \text{wrath}, \quad \text{constrained by}\;\Delta t \to 0$$ “Great wrath, because he knoweth that he hath but a short time.” Plain English: Cast down and time-limited.
The wrath is precisely because access has been limited — the adversary's own behavior is evidence of the regime change.
Each one independently shows a sharp regime change at the Gospels/Acts boundary.
Same Master-Equation variable, two coordinate names: “entropy” in the physics frame, “sin” in the theological frame.
The article's claim: a single structural change (Spirit distribution) drives discontinuities in encounter frequency, intervention mode, language, and community resilience simultaneously.
Constrained Wrath (Revelation 12:12) “Great wrath, because he knoweth that he hath but a short time.” Cast down and time-limited; the article reads the wrath as evidence of the regime change.
The distribution shows phase transition at Gospels/Acts; scale falls as frequency rises.
The article's claim: Eden was the normal state, the OT was the damaged state, Pentecost was the restoration.
The article offers the regime model as the missing structural answer.
Read structurally: armor is required because the attack mode is now indirect / institutional.
The article's coupling-constant analysis sits inside this equation; σ gates the F (Faith) term against the χ substrate.

Logos Field ($\chi$) The pervasive substrate of coherent reality — the framework's $\chi$ field; the Master-Equation field whose value is total system coherence. (Earlier drafts denoted this with $\Lambda$; that symbol is now reserved for the cosmological constant.) Field ≠ coupling : this entry locks the substrate sense.
